instruction:
Let $ r(x)=\frac{p(x)}{q(x)}$, where $p$ and $q$ are polynomials of degrees $m$ and $n$ respectively.
(a) If $r(x) \to 0$ as $x \to \infty$, then [ANS] A. $m=n$  B. $m > n$  C. $m < n$  D. None of the above
(b) If $r(x) \to k$ as $x \to \infty$, with $k \not=0$, then [ANS] A. $m=n$  B. $m < n$  C. $m > n$  D. None of the above
